title: gonystylus calophylloides
intro: Gonystylus calophylloides, also known as the Forest Ironwood, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, particularly in Malaysia and Indonesia. It is a deciduous tree valued for its timber and medicinal properties. The bark and leaves of the Forest Ironwood have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits, including relieving respiratory issues and promoting overall well-being.
